page: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system includes 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is delivered at a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core and software compilation tests. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both small and large block reads, though large block write bandwidth remains in the average tier.

The hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from high-speed DDR5 RAM and dedicated compute resources. It excels in database operations, as evidenced by top-tier Redis SET throughput and strong Passmark database scores. The inclusion of Intel AMX instructions enables efficient low-latency LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing on medium-sized models. This instance represents a resource-dense option for enterprise databases, memory-cached applications, and large-scale data processing pipelines where high memory-to-vCPU ratios are required.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, though it lacks local storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong capabilities in LLM inference and database processing. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, and data processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-performance database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise databases and memory-intensive applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
512.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Best-fit for enterprise databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive data processing
